page: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a Cloud GPU server configured with an Intel Xeon 6448H processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU provides 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz with a shared allocation model. The system features 180.0 GB of system memory, yielding 11.25 GB per core. Graphics and parallel processing acceleration are delivered by a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU equipped with 48 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in specific workloads, particularly LLM inference. For a small 135M model, prompt processing reaches 13631.52 tokens per second and text generation achieves 610.8 tokens per second. With a medium 7B model, prompt processing registers at 6293.73 tokens per second and text generation at 153.1 tokens per second, both placing in the top tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top tier at 35.53 GB/sec and 15.59 GB/sec respectively. General CPU and memory benchmarks, such as the Passmark CPU score of 40930.36 and Passmark memory score of 2177.65, fall within the average range.

This server offers a specialized balance of high-capacity GPU memory and system RAM, making it a resource-dense option for memory-heavy acceleration tasks. However, the shared CPU allocation and 2.0 GHz base clock speed represent a tradeoff for workloads requiring dedicated, high-frequency CPU cores. Given the top-tier LLM inference speeds and substantial VRAM, this configuration is highly suited for artificial intelligence inference, machine learning model execution, data science pipelines, and memory-intensive GPU-accelerated database operations.
description: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a Cloud GPU server designed for memory-intensive accelerated workloads. It features an Intel Xeon 6448H processor with 16 vCPUs, 180.0 GB of system memory, and a 1200 GB NVMe SSD. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM. In benchmark testing, this server demonstrates top-tier performance for LLM inference, achieving 6293.73 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model. While its shared CPU architecture and 2.0 GHz clock speed deliver average general-purpose compute scores, its high VRAM and system memory density provide excellent qualitative efficiency for parallel processing. This server is optimized for AI inference, machine learning, and data science workloads.
og_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 16 vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM, optimized for top-tier LLM inference workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a Cloud GPU server with 16 vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for AI inference.
tagline: An accelerated cloud server featuring an NVIDIA L40S GPU and high-density memory for demanding artificial intelligence and inference workloads.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for parallel acceleration
Intel Xeon 6448H processor with 16 vCPUs and 180.0 GB system memory
1200 GB local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access
Top-tier LLM inference performance for small and medium models
Best suited for AI inference, machine learning, and data science pipelines
